source:
import React from 'react'; import { useRouter } from 'next/router'; import { Card, CardContent, CardHeader, CardTitle } from "@/components/ui/card"; import { GitMerge, ArrowRight, Shield, Zap, Info, AlertTriangle, Download, Send, Settings2, ChevronRight, Clock, CheckCircle2, Search, Eye, RefreshCw, FileJson, Trash2, Link2, ExternalLink } from "lucide-react"; import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"; import { Button } from "@/components/ui/button"; const Step = ({ n, title, desc }: { n: number; title: string; desc: React.ReactNode }) => (
{String(n).padStart(2, '0')}
{title}
{desc}
); const Badge = ({ label, color }: { label: string; color: string }) => (
{label}
); const SmartMigrateDoc: React.FC = () => { const router = useRouter(); return (
{/* Header */}

Smart Migrate

Real-time environment diffs ┬╖ Sync changes ┬╖ Content Type syncing ┬╖ Entry syncing

{/* What is Smart Migrate */}
What is Smart Migrate?

Smart Migrate is a powerful synchronization tool built specifically for Contentful environments. Instead of blindly overwriting data or manually moving JSON files, Smart Migrate compares two environments in real-time and shows you exactly what changed.

It connects via the CMA, compares Content Types and Entries between your Source and Target environments, and allows you to surgically sync over new or modified content. It handles recursive dependencies (like referenced assets or nested entries) automatically.

{[ { label: "Real-Time Diff", color: "bg-blue-500/10 text-blue-400" }, { label: "Lazy Loading", color: "bg-amber-500/10 text-amber-400" }, { label: "Show Changed Only", color: "bg-emerald-500/10 text-emerald-400" }, { label: "Smart Previews", color: "bg-violet-500/10 text-violet-400" }, { label: "Dependency Sync", color: "bg-pink-500/10 text-pink-400" }, ].map((b) => )}
{/* Prerequisites */} Before you start ΓÇö Prerequisites
1.
CMA Token configured. Ensure your Contentful Management API token is saved in Settings.
2.
Two environments. You need a Source environment (where the new content is) and a Target environment (where you want to move it to).
{/* Step-by-Step Guide */}
Step-by-Step Guide ΓÇö How to Sync Changes

The Smart Migrate flow is designed to be safe, fast, and transparent. Follow these steps to sync your environments.

Choose the Source Environment (e.g., staging) and the Target Environment (e.g., master). These can be in the same space or different spaces. } /> Press the Load Live Diff button. The app will connect to Contentful, perform a top-level comparison of all Content Types and Locales, and immediately show you the results without freezing your browser. } /> By default, the list is filtered to Changed. This means it hides any Content Types that are 100% identical (= EQUAL) in both environments. You will only see Content Types that are NEW, MODIFIED, or contain modified entries. } /> Each visible Content Type card shows its overall status. If there are differences in the schema, you will see a badge. If the schema is identical but the entries inside are different, the UI will automatically scan and flag that there are modified entries. } /> To see exactly what changed inside a Content Type, click the Show entries Γû╝ button. This triggers a lazy-load to fetch the exact differences in entries for just that Content Type. You will see NEW entries, MODIFIED entries (with a field-change count), and unmodified ones. } /> Check the boxes next to the Content Types or individual Entries you wish to migrate. If you select an entry that requires a specific Content Type schema, Smart Migrate will automatically select the required dependencies to prevent broken links in the target. } /> Scroll to the bottom to configure migration options like Include Assets, the Migration Mode (Upsert vs Skip), and whether to create a safety backup. } /> Click Start Migration. A real-time streaming console will appear, showing every single Content Type, Entry, and Asset as it is processed and published in the target environment. } />
{/* Anatomy of the Diff */}

Anatomy of the Smart Diff

NEW Content

Marked with a prominent green Γèò NEW badge. This means the item exists in the Source but does not exist at all in the Target. Migrating this will create a brand new Content Type or Entry in the Target environment.

MODIFIED Content

Marked with a yellow Γ£Ä MODIFIED badge. This means the item exists in both environments, but the fields or schema are different. For entries, the UI will tell you exactly how many fields differ (e.g., "2 fields changed").

DELETED Content

Marked with a red Γèù DELETED badge. This means the item exists in the Target environment but is missing from your Source environment. Note: The tool currently prevents selecting Deleted items to avoid accidentally wiping data from your Target. It serves purely as an informational warning that your Target has orphaned content.

EQUAL Content

Marked with a gray = EQUAL badge. The item is 100% identical in both environments. By default, the "Changed" filter hides these items to keep the interface clean and focused on action items.

{/* Performance */} Progressive Background Loading

Enterprise Contentful spaces can have thousands of entries. If Smart Migrate tried to download and compare all of them at once, the API would time out and the browser would crash.

To solve this, Smart Migrate uses Progressive Background Loading. When you first load the diff, it only compares the Content Type definitions (schemas) and counts the total entries. Then, silently in the background, it begins querying entries in small batches of 3 Content Types at a time.

If the background worker discovers that an "Equal" Content Type actually contains "Modified" entries inside, it will instantly pop that Content Type into your "Changed" view. This architecture guarantees infinite scale while keeping the UI snappy.

{/* Related Documentation */}

Related Documentation

{[ { label: "Smart Restore", desc: "For full environment cloning & backup restores", tab: "smart-restore" }, { label: "Backups History", desc: "View automatic safety snapshots", tab: "backups" }, ].map((link) => ( ))}
); }; export default SmartMigrateDoc;

Use `never` instead, this is useful when instantiating generic type parameters that you don't need to know the type of.
